# Phase 3 approval checklist

## Content model

- [ ] Public and internal content types are unambiguous.
- [ ] Metadata contains only fields used by a system or review process.
- [ ] IA placement is not duplicated in front matter.
- [ ] Risk tiers define review depth.
- [ ] Source-of-truth rules distinguish generated facts from authored explanation.

## Templates

- [ ] Landing, task, workflow, concept, troubleshooting, runbook, reference, and deprecation patterns are covered.
- [ ] Templates use standard Markdown and maintained Material primitives.
- [ ] No template requires a generic evidence rail, owner panel, screenshot manifest, or custom HTML.
- [ ] `/get-started/` begins from a blank brief and does not preserve the current onboarding sequence.

## Editorial and accessibility

- [ ] Titles are precise tasks or lookups.
- [ ] Product labels and source accuracy are reviewable.
- [ ] AI, evidence, confidence, null, zero, stale, and unavailable states are explicit.
- [ ] Procedures expose prerequisites, verification, failure, and escalation when risk requires them.
- [ ] Visual and link rules meet WCAG 2.2 AA authoring needs.
- [ ] Human editorial judgment is not replaced with exact-prose tests.
